openmeter/meter/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing meter.Service and meter.ManageService. Owns all DB reads/writes for meter entities, enforcing namespace isolation and soft-delete via DeletedAt.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping on every method
patterns.description: Mutating methods wrap their body in transaction.Run + entutils.TransactingRepo (or WithNoValue); read-only methods use entutils.TransactingRepo directly.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, a, func(ctx) (Meter, error) { return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx, repo *Adapter) (Meter, error) { ... }) })

patterns.name: Tx/WithTx/Self triad
patterns.description: Adapter implements entutils.TxCreator: Tx() hijacks via HijackTx+NewTxDriver, WithTx() builds txClient from raw config, Self() returns self. Omitting any breaks TransactingRepo rebinding.
patterns.example: func (a *Adapter) WithTx(ctx, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *Adapter { return &Adapter{db: db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()).Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Config-validated constructor
patterns.description: New(Config) validates Client and Logger before constructing; returns error on invalid config.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: var _ meter.Service = (*Adapter)(nil) asserts compile-time satisfaction; add similar assertions for new interfaces.

patterns.name: MapFromEntityFactory for all domain mapping
patterns.description: All query paths convert *db.Meter to meter.Meter via MapFromEntityFactory — never build domain objects inline from Ent entities.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt filter
patterns.description: Meters are soft-deleted by SetDeletedAt(clock.Now()); queries default to meterdb.DeletedAtIsNil() unless IncludeDeleted is set.

patterns.name: Typed error wrapping
patterns.description: db.IsConstraintError -> models.NewGenericConflictError; db.IsNotFound -> meter.NewMeterNotFoundError; otherwise fmt.Errorf-wrapped.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter struct, Config, constructor, and Tx/WithTx/Self triad.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting any of Tx/WithTx/Self breaks TransactingRepo for this adapter.

key_file_guides.role: Create/Update/Delete plus cross-entity checks (HasActiveFeatureForMeter, HasEntitlementForMeter, ListFeaturesForMeter); uses featureadapter.MapFeatureEntity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Cross-entity queries use entitlementdb/featuredb predicates — missing namespace Where clauses can leak cross-namespace data.

key_file_guides.role: ListMeters (pagination, filtering, ordering), GetMeterByIDOrSlug, and MapFromEntityFactory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The OrderBy switch must handle all meter.OrderBy constants or return GenericValidationError on default.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests using testutils.InitPostgresDB + Schema.Create on a real DB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use t.Context(); context.Background() breaks test-scoped cancellation and transaction cleanup.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly in a helper without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-carried transaction
Hand-writing SQL instead of using Ent query builders
Returning raw Ent entities instead of mapping via MapFromEntityFactory
Omitting DeletedAt IS NULL where soft-deleted meters should be excluded

decisions.decision: Adapter implements both meter.Service and meter.ManageService at the DB layer; service/ wraps it for business logic
decisions.rationale: Keeps persistence isolated; hooks, event publishing, and cross-domain validation live in service/ without mixing into SQL.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete via DeletedAt rather than hard DELETE
decisions.rationale: Meters referenced by historical billing/usage data must not be physically removed, preserving audit trails and FK integrity.

code_examples.scenario: Create a meter inside a transaction

openmeter/meter/httphandler.purpose: v1 HTTP handler layer for the meter domain. Exposes meter CRUD, query (JSON and CSV), subject list, and group-by endpoints via the httptransport.Handler pattern; delegates to meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector.

patterns.name: Handler interface + factory
patterns.description: Handler composes sub-handler interfaces (MeterHandler); New(...) builds the concrete struct; var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) asserts compliance.
patterns.example: func New(namespaceDecoder, customerService, meterService, streaming, subjectService, options...) Handler { return &handler{...} }

patterns.name: NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints with path params use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params]; the decoder receives parsed params alongside *http.Request.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved at decode time
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first and embeds the resolved namespace in the request struct — never passes raw http.Request to the service layer.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Req{}, err }

patterns.name: Mapping functions live in mapping.go
patterns.description: All api<->domain conversions (ToAPIMeter, ToAPIMeterQueryResult, toQueryParamsFromRequest) live in mapping.go; handler files must not inline conversions.

patterns.name: JSON-path validation before meter mutations
patterns.description: validateJSONPaths/validateGroupByJSONPaths call streaming.ValidateJSONPath before CreateMeter/UpdateMeter because ClickHouse rejects invalid paths at query time.

patterns.name: CSV response via commonhttp.CSVResponseEncoder
patterns.description: CSV endpoints return commonhttp.CSVResponse (Records() + FileName()); queryMeterCSVResult in query_csv.go implements it.

key_file_guides.role: Handler/MeterHandler interface, concrete handler struct, New constructor with all service deps.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new endpoint requires extending MeterHandler and implementing the method on *handler.

key_file_guides.role: Domain<->API converters and toQueryParamsFromRequest (resolves customer IDs, validates group-by, maps window timezone).
key_file_guides.watch_for: getFilterCustomer surfaces customer-not-found as models.NewGenericNotFoundError, not 500.

key_file_guides.role: CRUD handlers (List, Get, Create, Update, Delete) and validateJSONPaths helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateMeter fetches the current meter first to resolve ID; removing it breaks the update flow.

key_file_guides.role: QueryMeter (GET), QueryMeterPost (POST), ListSubjects, ListGroupByValues — delegate to streaming.Connector after resolving the meter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListGroupByValues defaults From to last 24 hours when both From and To are nil — keep it to avoid unbounded ClickHouse scans.

key_file_guides.role: CSV variants; queryMeterCSVResult.Records() builds header/data rows with optional subject display names.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Subject display names are best-effort; missing subjects fill empty string, not an error.

anti_patterns: Calling meter.ManageService or streaming.Connector in the decoder func — decoding must only extract/validate HTTP input
Inline type conversions in handler files instead of mapping.go
Skipping validateJSONPaths before CreateMeter/UpdateMeter — ClickHouse rejects invalid JSONPath at query time
Returning 500 for customer-not-found in filter resolution — return models.NewGenericNotFoundError
Using context.Background() instead of the request ctx

decisions.decision: handler holds both meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector directly
decisions.rationale: Query operations run directly against ClickHouse via Connector, bypassing the meter service to avoid double-hop latency.

decisions.decision: POST body query mapped via ToRequestFromQueryParamsPOSTBody before toQueryParamsFromRequest
decisions.rationale: A single shared params-to-streaming conversion path reduces divergence between GET and POST query semantics.

code_examples.scenario: Typical handler: decode path param + namespace, delegate, map response

openmeter/meter/mockadapter.purpose: In-memory mock of meter.Service and meter.ManageService for tests. Optionally syncs meters to PostgreSQL via SetDBClient to satisfy FK constraints on features.meter_id.

patterns.name: Two-level struct: adapter + manageAdapter
patterns.description: adapter implements meter.Service with an in-memory slice; manageAdapter embeds it and adds Create/Update/Delete/RegisterHooks, satisfying meter.ManageService.

patterns.name: New vs NewManage factory selection
patterns.description: Read-only tests call New(meters); mutation tests call NewManage(meters). Both validate input meters.

patterns.name: Optional PG sync via SetDBClient
patterns.description: Call adapter.SetDBClient(*entdb.Client) after New to make ReplaceMeters/CreateMeter persist rows to PG; PG sync happens before in-memory state updates.

patterns.name: ReplaceMeters for bulk test seeding
patterns.description: ReplaceMeters validates, syncs to PG if SetDBClient was set (reusing existing (namespace, key) rows), then updates in-memory state only after PG success.

patterns.name: Zero-page pagination returns full dataset
patterns.description: ListMeters returns all matching items when params.Page.IsZero(), skipping offset math.
patterns.example: if params.Page.IsZero() { return pagination.Result[meter.Meter]{Items: meters, TotalCount: len(meters)}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: In-memory adapter struct, New/NewManage constructors, SetDBClient, TestAdapter type alias.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestAdapter exposes adapter internals — use only for field inspection, not to bypass the Service interface.

key_file_guides.role: In-memory Create/Update/Delete; RegisterHooks is a noop.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateMeter/DeleteMeter match by ID or Key in the slice — both must match the namespace to avoid cross-namespace mutations.

key_file_guides.role: ListMeters (in-memory filter + pagination), GetMeterByIDOrSlug, ReplaceMeters (PG sync logic).
key_file_guides.watch_for: PG sync in ReplaceMeters reuses existing (namespace, key) rows; with shared template DBs, IDs may be silently remapped.

anti_patterns: Using mockadapter in production wiring — it is test-only
Calling adapter.meters directly from tests instead of the Service interface
Calling SetDBClient after meters are seeded without ReplaceMeters — existing in-memory meters won't sync to PG
Expecting RegisterHooks/RegisterPreUpdateMeterHook to fire — it is a noop in the mock
Using context.Background() in tests when t.Context() is available

decisions.decision: Optional PG sync via SetDBClient rather than always requiring a DB
decisions.rationale: Pure in-memory tests need no DB; only tests that also create features (FK on features.meter_id) need PG sync.

openmeter/meter/service.purpose: Business logic layer for the meter domain. Service delegates reads to the adapter; ManageService adds mutation orchestration — event-type validation, cross-domain conflict checks, pre-update hooks, and Watermill event publishing.

patterns.name: Service embeds adapter for reads
patterns.description: Service holds *adapter.Adapter and delegates ListMeters/GetMeterByIDOrSlug directly — no extra logic for reads.

patterns.name: ManageService embeds Service + adds mutation orchestration
patterns.description: ManageService embeds meter.Service, holds *adapter.Adapter separately for mutations, plus publisher, namespaceManager, eventTypeValidator, hooks.
patterns.example: type ManageService struct { meter.Service; hooks *models.ServiceHookRegistry[meter.Meter]; adapter *adapter.Adapter; publisher eventbus.Publisher }

patterns.name: Publish domain events after each successful mutation
patterns.description: After successful Create/Update/Delete, publish a typed event (NewMeterCreateEvent/Update/Delete) via eventbus.Publisher — never before the write.

patterns.name: Pre-update hook registry
patterns.description: RegisterHooks appends to the hooks registry; UpdateMeter calls s.hooks.PreUpdate before adapter.UpdateMeter for cross-domain validation.
patterns.example: if err := s.hooks.PreUpdate(ctx, &currentMeter); err != nil { return meter.Meter{}, err }

patterns.name: Cross-domain conflict checks before delete
patterns.description: DeleteMeter checks HasActiveFeatureForMeter and HasEntitlementForMeter via the adapter; returns GenericConflictError if active references exist.
patterns.example: if hasFeatures { return models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("meter has active features and cannot be deleted")) }

key_file_guides.role: ManageService Create/Update/Delete plus hook/event plumbing and all cross-domain validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateMeter calls namespaceManager.CreateNamespace as a side effect (TODO workaround) after the DB write; publishing events before the adapter mutation succeeds is a violation.

key_file_guides.role: Read-only Service wrapping the adapter; thin delegation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service is created inside NewManage via New(adapter) — do not instantiate Service separately when ManageService is needed.

anti_patterns: Adding DB queries directly to service.go or manage.go — persistence goes through the adapter
Publishing events before the adapter mutation succeeds
Skipping pre-update hooks in UpdateMeter — downstream billing/entitlement hooks rely on them
Adding adapter-only methods to the read-only Service interface

decisions.decision: Service layer publishes Watermill events after mutations rather than the adapter
decisions.rationale: Adapter is pure persistence; event publishing is a business concern that keeps the adapter testable without an event bus.

decisions.decision: ManageService holds a direct *adapter.Adapter in addition to the embedded meter.Service
decisions.rationale: Cross-domain checks (HasActiveFeatureForMeter, HasEntitlementForMeter) are adapter methods not on the meter.Service interface.

code_examples.scenario: Delete a meter with conflict guard and post-delete event
